nodes.summary: Routed the Space Invaders prompt to archetype "gallery_shooter" (live-verified in templates/genres.json archetypes[]). Physics: side view, no gravity, continuous L/R slide + straight-up fire. scoringModel=bounded-threshold (explicit per-wave target: clear the formation before it lands; the prompt's "wave after wave" is an explicit level sequence, NOT cut). scopeCut=7 standard over-scope systems (no multiple-levels cut — prompt requested it); mustPreserve captures the contested column-clearing decision so W1 keeps round one a real escalating shooter, not a tutorial.

nodes.label: w1-design

reads.preview:
{
  "prompt": "A classic space invaders game where I slide a little laser cannon left and right along the bottom and fire straight up at a descending grid of marching aliens, clearing wave after wave before the swarm reaches the ground.",
  "archetype": "gallery_shooter",
  "coreLoop": "The player slides a laser cannon left and right along the bottom and fires straight up to thin a descending grid of marching aliens wave after wave; an enemy projectile, a marcher touching the cannon's row, or the swarm reaching the ground ends the run, and the next wave restarts the march tighter and faster.",
  "coreVerb": "shoot",
  "physicsProfile": {
    "hasGravity": false,
    "perspective": "side",
    "movementType": "continuous"
  },
  "reasoning": "Side-view perspective, no gravity, continuous left-right cannon slide with straight-up fire onto a step-march alien formation — the gallery_shooter:fixed-axis KEY QUESTION fires (fixed-axis slide-and-shoot at a marching formation that speeds up as it thins).",
  "confidence": "high",
  "scoringModel": "bounded-threshold",
  "scopeCut": [
    "multiplayer / networking",
    "save/load + persistence (single-session runs only)",
    "in-game shop / economy / account login / monetization",
    "online leaderboards / cloud sync",
    "story / dialogue / cutscenes",
    "mobile / touch / VR controls (desktop keyboard only)",
    "procedural level generation (waves are hand-tuned, not random)"
  ],
  "mustPreserve": "which column to clear as the formation steps down — trading fire rate for lateral cover and racing the march before any row reaches the cannon",
  "coreFantasy": "a lone cannon holding off the alien march"
}

nodes.summary: Filled spec/gdd.md for the gallery_shooter prompt "Lone Cannon" — win-lose fixed-axis with a cover-erosion hook (DestructibleBunker eroded by BOTH player fire AND BulletHellEmitter bombs). Three milestones (core verb → contested-decision beat with idempotent + bounded score → earned win + terminal lose + wave-loop advance), each 4 assertions, every mechanic bound to a resolving capabilities.json id, zero custom[] gaps.

nodes.summary: Produced spec/blueprint.json for gs01 (Lone Cannon, gallery_shooter, win-lose): full gameplay sections (meta, entities, mechanics, controls, genre, controlScheme, effects, systems, custom, win/lose, config, assetList, milestones, layout, coupling, feasibility, referenceSolution, acceptanceCriteria, declaredRanges) + chromeDemands pointer. All bindings resolve in gallery_shooter/capabilities.json + events.catalog.json; feasibility PASSes wave 1 (clearTime 16.4s < descentTime 52.2s) and the wave-3 climax (clearTime 22.7s < descentTime 27.2s); coupling shows the formation on every path to formation_clear. No authored maxScore integer (engine-derives via registerScorable); AC-M2-A3 uses the SYMBOL 'maxScore' with atMost. chrome sections (hud/coaching/intro/shell/modes) are intentionally OMITTED per the v1.6 dependency-separation topology — only a thin chromeDemands field is written for the downstream chrome producers.

nodes.label: asset
nodes.summary: Authored 8 creative image prompts (one per non-audio blueprint slot, in assetList order) + an 8-role "Indigo Swarm" palette for Lone Cannon (gallery_shooter). All 5 Required bars pass: single-subject per prompt; global style anchor ("pixel-art retro arcade style, solid pixel fills, warm-cool atmospheric contrast") verbatim in every prompt; each prompt names its own role hex by name; all role colors distinct (cyan/teal/magenta gradient across the three alien tiers per the design's explicit demand, plus warm amber player, pale amber projectile, hot-red hazard, steel-blue obstacle, deep-indigo background); no technical/negative scaffolding.

nodes.summary: Authored the guidance section for Lone Cannon (gallery_shooter:fixed-axis) bound to the seeded surface — 5 coaching tips (M1 verb teach safely pre-threat; M2 first-kill scoring + back-row priority, first-bunker cover erosion, first-bomb threat intro, first-hit HP intro), 3 overlay stingers (wave.cleared celebration, status=won victory, status=lost cannon-fallen), and 1 diegetic world-cue (beacon at formation_clear). Every trigger resolves to a handle in blueprint.contracts.guidance.bind (player.shot / enemy.died / bunker.damaged / pattern.fired / player.damaged / wave.cleared events; waveIndex / player.health / status observables; formation_clear.position entity) — no surface gaps, all cues decay via preferred dismissOn (success event) with durationMs fallback where no natural event exists, verb taught safely before any threat, no baseline duplication.

nodes.label: w2-scaffold

reads.preview:
# W3a — Art Director (Lone Cannon, gs01)

palette: Indigo Swarm (8 role entries; player/projectile/hazard/enemy_row1/enemy_row2/enemy_row3/obstacle/background)
no blocked slots; all 8 non-audio slots authored in blueprint assetList order.
three enemy tier roles (enemy_row1 cyan / enemy_row2 teal / enemy_row3 magenta) split so each tier reads apart by hue per the design's explicit "cyan→teal→magenta gradient that rises by row" + the same-family tier-distinction rule; the artStyle names three distinct hues for the three tiers, so a single palette.roles.enemy entry would have collapsed two of the three tiers.
style anchor verbatim in every prompt: "pixel-art retro arcade style, solid pixel fills, warm-cool atmospheric contrast".
no ground tile in this archetype (gallery_shooter void) — closest surface = obstacle bunker; WCAG luminance contrast vs background (#0a0a2e L≈0.012) is ~8.6, well above the 1.5 floor.
no reserved accent appears in the background palette or background prompt.

/**
 * Level Manager — engine seam re-export (KEEP).
 *
 * The pure level-order logic was RELOCATED to the engine-agnostic CONTRACT home
 * `@contract/LevelManager` (the shared top-level `templates/core-contract/`,
 * imported by every engine). This file re-exports it so every existing
 * `import { LevelManager } from '../LevelManager'` consumer (the core UI scenes,
 * the platformer BaseLevelScene) resolves unchanged.
 */
export { LevelManager } from '@contract/LevelManager';

nodes.summary: W2 Scaffold (Coder, gs01 — Lone Cannon) BUILT GREEN. archetype=gallery_shooter (subgenre fixed-axis); pre-stage verified (package.json + src/main.ts present); window.__GAME__ exposed via installGameHook (src/hook.ts). Weaved the entity-binding into src/levels/level1.json (player.id+assetSlot+displayWidth/Height, the 5×11 formation grid + rows_template from blueprint.layout.threats[], the 4 bunkers from blueprint.layout.platforms[], all 14 effects + all 5 systems incl BulletHellEmitter+DestructibleBunker, backgroundSlot 'background'). Populated gameConfig literals (controlsHelp=blueprint.controls, failModel='health', objective=winCondition.description, shell/guidance/sound chrome sections projected from blueprint) and corrected drift in the driver-projected numerical keys (invulnerableTime 800→1200, bulletSpeed 520→480, points 10→30, baseStepMs 600→580, floorStepMs 60→75). Set package.json.name='lone-cannon'. Derived index.json (8 slots from blueprint.assetList[]: cannon/alienRow1/alienRow2/alienRow3/bunker/bullet/enemyBomb/background — valid vs packages/skills/scaffold/index.schema.json). Wrote STRUCTURE.md (Controls / Scenes / Entities / Systems / Behaviors / State&Event map / Test hook / Assets / Build / Notes; TODO-W4 marker for custom[]). Wrote MEMORY.w2.md (per-node fragment). npm run build exits 0 (tsc --noEmit clean; vite build emits dist/index.html + 1.6MB bundle).

nodes.summary: M2 implemented as a no-op delta (blueprint.custom[] is empty for this design, same as M1). The data-driven DataShooterScene + materialized src/levels/level1.json already ship the entire M2 surface verbatim — the 55-marcher formation, the ProjectilePool bullet↔enemy overlap with the isDead guard, and the BaseGameScene.onEnemyKilled → utils.addScore credit path. Build green (tsc --noEmit clean + vite build emits the 1.6 MB bundle). Discovered one critical engine gap for VERIFY-2 to handle: the gallery_shooter engine never calls utils.registerScorable on member placement (DataShooterScene.spawnMember sets sprite.__points but never registers it into the maxScore registry key), so __GAME__.maxScore = 0 and the M2-A3 bounded-score invariant `score atMost maxScore` will evaluate `score <= 0` after the first kill. Documented in MEMORY.w4-M2.md with the verbatim seam, the path to fix (a single-line add in spawnMember), and the SKILL §2.1 blueprint-engine-gap classification.
issues: blueprint-engine-gap: gallery_shooter engine does not register maxScore on member placement; __GAME__.maxScore returns 0; M2-AC-A3 (score atMost maxScore) will fail VERIFY-2 unless the engine spawnMember path is patched to call utils.registerScorable(this, sprite.__points). Documented in MEMORY.w4-M2.md as a critical discovery for VERIFY-2's bounded self-fix.

nodes.label: w4-execute-m3
nodes.summary: W4 EXECUTE M3 (Lone Cannon · gs01) — wrote the per-node fragment MEMORY.w4-M3.md (284 lines / 18.5 KB) documenting the verbatim M3 acceptance-criteria paths (final-wave win via WaveLoop → onLevelComplete → registry.set('status','won'); lethal-blow via BulletHellEmitter overlap → player.takeDamage → kill → onPlayerDeath → registry.set('status','lost'); wave-loop-advance via WaveLoop.update; bunker-erosion via DestructibleBunker overlap → consumeBullet + erodeCell). Zero src/** writes — blueprint.custom[]=[] so the data-driven DataShooterScene + the materialized src/levels/level1.json (W2's weave) already resolve every M3 mechanic (WaveLoop / BulletHellEmitter / DestructibleBunker / FormationMarch / ProjectilePool) and the event→effect bindings. Build passed green on first attempt (tsc --noEmit clean; vite build emitted dist/index.html + 1.6 MB JS bundle).
